message:
Standardize formatting across end-to-end test suites

Applies consistent rustfmt styling to five E2E test files for shell completions,
history backups, linting, upgrade/version commands, and defer functionality.

Formatting changes applied uniformly:

Assert macro formatting:
- Split two-argument assert! calls with format strings onto multiple lines,
  placing the format string and its arguments on separate lines from the
  condition. This improves readability when assertion messages are long
  and makes git diffs cleaner when only the message changes.
- Example: assert!(x, "message: {}", y) becomes:
  assert!(
      x,
      "message: {}",
      y
  );

Import ordering:
- Reorder use statements to match rustfmt canonical ordering
  (e.g., BrWorkspace before run_br in e2e_history.rs)

Inline format arguments:
- Convert format!("{:?}", var) to format!("{var:?}") style throughout
- Apply to assertion messages like "should have multiple backups: {backups:?}"
  instead of "should have multiple backups: {:?}", backups
- This Rust 2021+ feature improves readability and reduces visual noise

Function call formatting:
- Consolidate unnecessarily multi-line function calls to single lines
  where they fit within line limits (e.g., run_br calls in e2e_history.rs)

Files modified:
- tests/e2e_completions.rs: 15 assert macro reformats
- tests/e2e_history.rs: Import reorder, 6 assert reformats, 4 inline format args
- tests/e2e_lint.rs: 10 assert macro reformats
- tests/e2e_upgrade.rs: 5 assert macro reformats
- tests/e2e_defer.rs: 2 assert macro reformats

No behavioral changes; all tests produce identical results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
